title: Ukraine Achieves World First: USV-Launched Drone Intercepts Russian Shahed at Sea
detail: Ukrainian forces scored a world first by launching a Sting interceptor drone from an unmanned surface vessel (USV) to shoot down a Russian Shahed drone over the Black Sea. The breakthrough combines two of Ukraine's signature drone innovations — sea drone operations and aerial drone interception — into a single platform, extending air defense coverage to maritime assets. The development reflects Ukraine's accelerating UAV technology development and its ability to deny Russia air supremacy even at sea.

media.caption: Ukraine's drone warfare innovations now include USV-launched aerial interceptors

title: 153 Combat Engagements; Russia Deploys 9,360 Drones in 24 Hours; Losses Exceed 1.318M
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 153 combat engagements across all frontline sectors over 24 hours — one of the highest single-day counts of 2026. Russian forces deployed approximately 9,360 kamikaze drones including FPV and Shahed-type UAVs, conducted 68 airstrikes with 216 guided aerial bombs, and fired 3,404 artillery rounds in the period. Cumulative Russian personnel losses exceeded 1,318,000, with approximately 1,070 casualties in the single period. Intense pressure continued in the Pokrovsk, Kurakhove, and Kupyansk directions as Russia's spring offensive maintained high tempo.

media.caption: Ukrainian forces face 153 combat engagements as Russia sustains its spring offensive
